STOCK TRAILER WAS FLIPPED. A TRUCK WAS PUSHED 200 YARDS AND THROUGH A FENCE. AN OLD OUTBUILDING WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. ALL ON THE FARM REACHED SAFETY AND THERE WERE NO INJURIES. NO DEATHS. THIS FARM WAS ABOUT ONE MILE NORTH OF WHERE THE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. GENERALLY 9 MILES NORTH OF KILLDEER (MOBILE HOME) TO 10 MILES NORTH OF KILLDEER (FARM).

TORNADO CLIPPED THE SOUTHEAST CORNER OF THE CITY OF HEBRON, WITHIN CITY LIMITS, AS IT MOVED NORTH. MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES IN A TREE ROW. NO OTHER DAMAGE REPORTED. VIDEO OF THE TORNADO WAS SENT TO THE NWS AND WE TALKED AT LENGTH WITH THE EYE WITNESS / VIDEOGRAPHER. INTERESTINGLY, A TORNADO MOVED THROUGH THIS SAME SOUTHEAST CORNER OF THIS SMALL CITY BACK ON AUGUST 24, 2006.

ESTIMATED 85 MPH WIND. DAMAGE TO A MEDIUM SIZED WOODEN OUTBUILDING ON A FARM. PART OF BUILDING SHIFTED OFF FOUNDATION, FRONT DOOR TORN OFF, BACK WALL BLOWN OUT. THIS WAS ALONG A LONG LINE OF GENERALLY 65 TO 85 MPH THUNDERSTORM WINDS. APEX OF WORST DAMAGE - THIS LOCATION, THROUGH STANTON, TO ABOUT 6 MILES NORTH OF WASHBURN, TO HECKERS / NEW JOHNS LAKES. MICROBURST WINDS OF 130 MPH OCCURRED 4 NNW OF STANTON AT 1230 AM CDT.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 2 SSE UNDERWOOD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA. LARGE SWATH (ABOUT 1 MILE LONG) OF EXTENSIVE THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE FROM AN INTENSE SQUALL LINE THAT MOVED THROUGH THE AREA DURING THE EARLY MORNING HOURS ON JUNE 29TH. 10 POWER LINES DOWNED AND MANY TREES TORN APART AND/OR STRIPPED OF BARK.

AN INTENSE MICROBURST OCCURRED AT THIS LOCATION WITH ESTIMATED 130 MPH THUNDERSTORM WINDS. EVERY BUILDING ON THE PROPERTY WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED, SOME DESTROYED. THE FARM HOUSE WAS VERY HEAVILY DAMAGED WITH THE ROOF TORN FROM THE HOME. FOUR PEOPLE INSIDE SOUGHT SHELTER IN THE BASEMENT AND WERE NOT INJURED. LARGE DIAMETER TREES WERE SNAPPED. OF THE PROPERTY DAMAGE SURVEYED ACROSS MERCER AND MCLEAN COUNTIES THIS WAS THE ABSOLUTE WORST BY FAR.
LATE REPORT. TIME RADAR ESTIMATED.
NDOT WIND EQUIPMENT.

*** 1 FATAL, 4 INJ *** THERE WAS A FATALITY, A 42 YEAR OLD MALE, AT THIS LOCATION NEXT TO HECKERS LAKE IN MCLEAN COUNTY, VERY NEAR THE BURLEIGH AND SHERIDAN COUNTY LINE. ABOUT 30 CAMPING TRAILERS DAMAGED, SOME DESTROYED, AS THEY OVERTURNED AND ROLLED IN AN ESTIMATED 80 MPH THUNDERSTORM WIND. 4 OTHERS WERE INJURED IN THE CAMPING AREAS AROUND HECKERS AND NEW JOHNS LAKES.

EMMONS CO. SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED EXTENSIVE WIND DAMAGE FROM BADGER BAY TO JUST NORTH OF THE HAZELTON RECREATIONAL AREA. AT BADGER BAY A 5TH WHEEL CAMPER WAS TIPPED OVER. 5 N OF BADGER BAY AN UNOCCUPIED 16X72 MOBILE HOME WAS TIPPED OVER, ROLLED, AND DESTROYED. NORTH OF THE HAZELTON REC AREA, A POLE BARN AND SEVERAL SMALLER SHEDS WERE DESTROYED. ALL DEBRIS WAS BLOWN TO THE NORTHEAST, SOME AS FAR AS 1/4 MILE. BASED ON THE DAMAGE, WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH.

MORTON COUNTY EM REPORTED EXTENSIVE T-STORM WIND DAMAGE AT FORT RICE AND SOUTH OF FORT RICE. SEVERAL GARAGES WERE RIPPED FROM FOUNDATIONS, AN UNOCCUPIED MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN DOWN A HILL, AND A TRAVEL TRAILER RV WAS BLOWN INTO THE RIVER. NO REPORTS OF INJURIES OR FATALITIES. LSR FROM SATURDAY, JULY 14. CROSSED ND 200 AND ON SOUTH SIDE TWO OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED. CROPS DAMAGED / DESTROYED. TREES SNAPPED. SECOND VISIBLE FROM 45 MILES AWAY. SECOND ONE EF0. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 5 MINUTES.

LSR FROM SATURDAY JULY 14. OVER A DISKED AREA OF LAND SO WAS BLACK WITH DIRT AND DUST AND VISIBLE FOR 45 MILES. ABOUT 35 HAY BALES REMOVED. ESTIMATED EF0 AND FROM THAT ESTIMATED WINDS 85 MPH. NO OTHER DAMAGE. ABOUT 300 YARDS WIDE AND ON THE GROUND FOR ONE MILE, ALL IN DUNN COUNTY (VERY CLOSE TO MERCER COUNTY LINE). DUST HELD IN THE AIR LONG AFTER THE FUNNEL DISSIPATED. SECOND OF TWO TORNADOES IN THIS AREA. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 13 MINUTES.
